steps.title: Set up the inequality for total cost with tax
steps.content:
Let $n$ = number of tablets. Each tablet costs \$170. After adding 7% tax, the total cost per tablet is $170 \times 1.07 = 181.90$. The school's total cost is $181.90n$, and this must not exceed \$3,400:
$$181.90n \leq 3400$$

steps.title: Solve for the maximum number of tablets
steps.content:
Divide both sides by $181.90$:
$$n \leq \frac{3400}{181.90} = 18.682...$$
Since the school can only order a whole number of tablets, round down to $n = 18$.

steps.title: Verify the answer
steps.content:
Check that 18 tablets fit within budget and 19 exceeds it:
  • 18 tablets: $18 \times 181.90 = 3274.20$ (within \$3,400 limit) ✓
  • 19 tablets: $19 \times 181.90 = 3456.10$ (exceeds \$3,400 limit) ✗
The maximum number of tablets the school can order is 18.
